title: BitNet-TT kernel-level optimization (session 2)
goal: Push past the Python-level ceiling with kernel-adjacent work. Target: p50 <=11.5 ms (>=87 t/s) AND PCC >=0.982. At minimum ship one change that moves either axis measurably.
constraints: tt-metal changes allowed but require rebuild; isolate to additive ops or feature flags if possible.
PCC must not regress beyond -0.002 under any circumstance.
Speed regressions >0.2 ms block unless offset by PCC >=0.003 or match ratio >=2x (same exception as session 1).
All measurements on TT server via ssh TT; code sync via git.

stories.title: cos/sin device-side embedding lookup in trace
acceptanceCriteria: generator_batch32 decode step replaces per-step cos/sin H2D copies with ttnn.embedding lookups against rotary_setup.cos_matrix / sin_matrix inside the trace.
Trace re-captures cleanly and execute_trace completes on p150a without Python errors.
Post-change bench_batch32 --max-new 128 decode_tps does NOT regress vs 71.89 (tolerance +/- 0.3 t/s); target +2-3 t/s.
Post-change bench_accuracy --decode-steps 32 PCC stays within 0.002 of 0.9807.

stories.title: nlp_create_qkv_heads_boltz drop-in experiment
acceptanceCriteria: attention._forward_decode_1bkd path gains a feature flag choosing between nlp_create_qkv_heads_decode (current) and nlp_create_qkv_heads_boltz (Blackhole variant).
Both paths produce numerically equivalent Q/K/V (PCC >= 0.9999 vs current) on p150a.
Pick the faster one as the default based on measured decode_tps on bench_batch32 --max-new 128.
stories.notes: If boltz variant is a superset OR strict win, integrate. If it regresses or is equivalent, document as a dead-end microbench and leave current op in place.

stories.title: Selective bf16 lm_head weight option for PCC boost
acceptanceCriteria: BITNET_LM_HEAD_DTYPE=bf16 continues to work and is measured at 128 tok / 32 step on the current HEAD.
Document whether bf16 lm_head becomes the recommendation when PCC is prioritized (decision goes into docs/ or README).
stories.notes: This is a documentation-grade story. The env override already exists. Only needs a fresh 128-tok measurement against the new baseline and a written recommendation.

stories.title: Regression + multi-prompt validation after kernel changes
acceptanceCriteria: Final HEAD passes bench_batch32 --max-new 128 and bench_accuracy --decode-steps 32 on p150a.
bench_accuracy_multi across 5 prompts keeps argmax match at 5/5 and mean PCC >=0.93.
README.md / TODO.md record the new baseline and what changed this iteration.
Final commits pushed to origin/main and synced on TT server.
